cli/slash-commands.title: Slash Commands Reference
cli/slash-commands.description: Slash commands are typed inside the TIMPS CLI session prompt.

contributing.title: Contributing
contributing.description: TIMPS is open source and welcomes contributions.

intro.title: Introduction
intro.description: TIMPS is a persistent cognitive partner that remembers, evolves, and builds with you across every session.

mcp/tools.title: MCP Tools Reference
mcp/tools.description: The timps-mcp server exposes 46 tools to any MCP-compatible client (Claude Code, Cursor, Windsurf, Continue).

memory/architecture.title: Memory Architecture
memory/architecture.description: The TIMPS 3-layer memory system is the core differentiator. All three layers persist to disk and survive process restarts.

memory/episodic.title: Episodic Memory
memory/episodic.description: Episodic memory is an append-only log of every TIMPS session. It answers: "What did we work on last week?"

memory/semantic.title: Semantic Memory
memory/semantic.description: Semantic memory holds distilled facts about the project — patterns, architectural decisions, constraints, and preferences. It persists indefinitely and is retrieved by relevance.

memory/working.title: Working Memory
memory/working.description: Working memory is the current session state — what the agent is doing right now. It's reset at the start of each new session.

skills/creating.title: Creating Skills
skills/creating.description: Skills are Markdown files with a metadata header. Adding one to the registry makes it available to all TIMPS users via /skills install.

skills/installing.title: Installing Skills
skills/installing.description: Skills are saved to ~/.timps/skills/.md and automatically loaded every time TIMPS starts.

skills/overview.title: Skills Overview
skills/overview.description: Skills are Markdown documents that inject domain knowledge into the TIMPS agent's system prompt. Install a skill once and it's automatically included every time TIMPS starts in that project.

vscode/memory-view.title: Memory Layers View
vscode/memory-view.description: The Memory Layers view is a native VS Code tree view in the TIMPS sidebar that shows all three memory layers live.

vscode/overview.title: VS Code Extension Overview
vscode/overview.description: The TIMPS — AI Coding Agent extension brings the full TIMPS experience into VS Code.
